mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy rates continue to rise.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the policy rate is restrictive enough to compress valuation multiples quickly, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if crossings above 4.5% do not produce lower 5-day forward returns than non-crossing months at p <= 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth with UNRATE below 4.0% signals strong household income and low layoff risk, supporting consumption-linked earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has a 60-day hit rate below 50% or an average 60-day return not above the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal consumer demand, which lifts near-term revenue expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes have Rank IC below 0.03 versus 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below 1300 thousand units, housing construction demand is contracting, weakening cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in INDPRO combined with rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals stagflation pressure that squeezes margins and valuations, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0, CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0, and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not lower mean 20-day returns relative to periods with rising INDPRO by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth above 2.0% signals broad real demand strength that raises aggregate earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is not associated with higher 60-day returns than GDPC1 growth below 2.0% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase greater than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% signals recession risk under tight policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if this joint trigger produces nonnegative average 5-day forward returns or a negative-return hit rate below 55% in walk-forward evaluation.
